body:
## Verdict

agent_supports: yes
daemon_capability: full; driver_capability: ready

## Reasoning

This is the smallest tool-less round-trip that pins the terminal-text end signal: the assistant produces a final message and the turn closes with no pending tool call or question, so the classifier drives workingâ†’ready. It overlaps intentionally with basic-turn but focuses on the end-signal mechanism rather than the broader happy-path lifecycle.

**agent_supports: yes.** Codex CLI 0.130.0 completes a tool-less single turn and writes the per-turn rollout JSONL the daemon tails. The terminal end signal codex emits is a `task_complete` `event_msg` line at the close of the turn â€” there is no tool call or question pending, so the turn settles on the final assistant text. The committed `regression/baseline-hello` fixture is a captured headless single-turn round-trip demonstrating exactly this shape.

**daemon_capability: full; driver_capability: ready.** The codex adapter watches session rollouts via `FilesUnderRoot{Dir: sessionsDir(), Parser: JSONLineParser{...}}` (agent.go). The JSONL parser (parser.go) maps the `task_complete` `event_msg` to `EventType: "turn_done"`, so `IsAgentDone()` fires through the primary path and the classifier (rule 2b) transitions workingâ†’ready once the terminal text turn closes. Because this cell carries no tool call, the `HasOpenToolCall` guard in `IsAgentDone` never blocks the settle, and because no question is pending the classifier never diverts to waiting. Parser unit tests `TestParser_FullTranscript_EndDetection` and `TestParser_TurnCompleteEmitsTurnDone` cover the turn-end detection this cell asserts. The coverage matrix already records codex/turn-end-terminal-text as yes/yes ("task_complete handled").

The behavior is recorded with the INTERACTIVE driver, not headless `codex exec`: a single-shot `exec` turn is too short for the daemon to observe the readyâ†’workingâ†’ready arc (the process exits before the workingâ†’ready settle, surfacing as process_exited rather than a turn-end transition). The interactive REPL stays alive across the one turn, so the daemon observes the full workingâ†’ready settle on the live rollout session, then process_exited only at REPL teardown â€” the same shape that lets basic-turn and multi-turn-conversation pass.

No carve-outs apply: codex assigns turn boundaries cleanly via `task_complete` (unlike aider, whose idle-flush turn-end carries ~5s latency and is recorded as `partial` in the matrix).
caveats: Overlaps heavily with `basic-turn`. The two cells differ in intent: basic-turn asserts the overall happy-path lifecycle (session detection + readyâ†’workingâ†’ready + non-zero tokens); turn-end-terminal-text specifically asserts that the terminal-text end signal closes the turn with no tool call or question pending. The recipe is a deliberately tool-less prompt eliciting a plain text answer with no trailing question mark, to keep the cell focused.

The scenario intent allows a waiting settle if codex's classifier reads a trailing cue. Eliciting a declarative answer with NO trailing question mark and NO tool call keeps the turn on the ready path; if a future codex release adds a terminal-text waiting heuristic this cell would surface it as drift.

sources.note: FilesUnderRoot + JSONLineParser transport; ExactName process match
task_complete event_msg -> turn_done so IsAgentDone fires; no tool_use in this cell so HasOpenToolCall never blocks the settle
Rule 2b: when IsAgentDone() is true and not waiting for user input, working/waiting -> ready
committed captured headless single-turn round-trip fixture
